definition.summary: Breakpoints define the viewport-width thresholds at which layout changes occur. They are primitive tokens with no semantic tier - components reference hds.breakpoints.* in JavaScript and the documented px values in CSS media queries.

rules.rule: JavaScript breakpoint checks must reference hds.breakpoints.* values - no magic numbers.
CSS custom properties cannot be used in @media min-width queries - they resolve after the cascade runs. Use the raw px values documented by the token.
All responsive CSS must be mobile-first: base styles target xs (375px), overrides use min-width queries at sm/md/lg/xl.

usage.condition: JavaScript component logic (isMobile check, column count)
CSS media query - single-column to two-column layout
CSS media query - sidebar layout activates

examples.name: CSS - @media min-width
UTILITY LAYER - Tailwind responsive prefix
examples.code: {/* grid-cols-1 is the mobile-first base */}

examples.name: JAVASCRIPT - token reference

avoid: Using @media max-width - the system is mobile-first, all responsive overrides are min-width.
Using CSS custom properties in @media min-width queries - they resolve after the cascade.
Hardcoding px values in JavaScript breakpoint checks - always reference hds.breakpoints.*.

breakpoints.name: xs
breakpoints.notes: iPhone mini baseline. Floor for all mobile layouts.

breakpoints.name: sm
breakpoints.notes: Large phone / landscape. Matches the utility-layer responsive scale.

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.notes: Mobile/tablet boundary for shell-responsive composition and JS layout checks.

breakpoints.name: lg
breakpoints.notes: Desktop. HDS sidebar layout activates here.
